member.name: T:PSParser.StringFolder
member.summary: Statically folds string expressions to their literal value. Handles direct literals and + concatenations of literals.

member.name: M:PSParser.StringFolder.TryFold(PSParser.Expression,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Tries to fold an expression to a literal string value. Pass (mapping $name → value) to enable constant propagation across variable assignments, e.g. $a="Ams"; $b="iSc"; $a+$b → "AmsiSc".

member.name: T:PSParser.AmsiBypassDetector
member.summary: Detects AMSI bypass attempts in PowerShell. Combines AST visitor pattern with raw source scanning to handle obfuscated code.

member.name: M:PSParser.AmsiBypassDetector.ScanSource(System.String)
member.summary: Scans raw PowerShell source for AMSI patterns. Works even when the parser cannot handle the syntax (e.g. [Ref].Assembly...). Call this before or after visiting the AST.

members.member: Scans the full raw source text for each StringIndicator. Catches bare-word AMSI names that aren't inside quoted strings (e.g. C# code inside PS here-strings, unquoted function arguments).

member.name: M:PSParser.AmsiBypassDetector.ExtractReplaceChainStrings(System.String)
member.summary: Evaluates $var = 'init'; $var = $var.replace('x','y') chains in source order. Catches patterns like: $s='hello, world'; $s=$s.replace('he','a')... → 'amsi.dll'

member.name: M:PSParser.AmsiBypassDetector.ExtractVariableFoldedStrings(System.String)
member.summary: Source-level constant propagation: resolves $var assignments to literals, then folds any variable+variable or variable+literal concatenations. Catches patterns like: $a="Ams"; $b="iSc"; ... GetBytes($a+$b+$c+$d)

members.member: Finds all "{N}" -f args expressions in source and returns the evaluated strings.
Captures the args string starting at 'start', stopping at unmatched ')' or end-of-statement.
Evaluates a PS string expression: handles literals, + concat, and -f format operator.

member.name: T:PSParser.AstNode
member.summary: Baza dla wszystkich węzłów AST

member.name: T:PSParser.Expression
member.summary: Wyrażenie w AST

member.name: T:PSParser.Statement
member.summary: Statement w AST

member.name: T:PSParser.InterpolationSegment
member.summary: Segment interpolacji w stringach

member.name: T:PSParser.StringEvaluator
member.summary: Safe string evaluator - rozwijanie stringów bez execution

member.name: M:PSParser.StringEvaluator.EvaluateExpandableString(PSParser.StringLiteral)
member.summary: Bezpiecznie rozwijamy expandowalny string

member.name: M:PSParser.StringEvaluator.UnquoteString(System.String)
member.summary: Usuwamy cudzysłowy z stringa

member.name: M:PSParser.StringEvaluator.TryDecodeBase64(System.String)
member.summary: Próbujemy dekodować łańcuchy Base64

member.name: M:PSParser.StringEvaluator.DeobfuscateNestedEncoding(System.String)
member.summary: Rozwijamy zagnieżdżone kodowanie (Base64 -> Base64 -> ...)

member.name: T:PSParser.ObfuscationDetector
member.summary: Detektor obfuscacji - identyfikuje techniki ukrycia

member.name: T:PSParser.ObfuscationIndicator
member.summary: Raport z analizy obfuscacji

member.name: T:PSParser.PSParser
member.summary: Parser dla PowerShela - konwertuje tokeny na AST

member.name: M:PSParser.PSParser.Parse
member.summary: Parsuje kod i zwraca AST

member.name: M:PSParser.PSParser.ExtractInterpolationSegments(System.String)
member.summary: Wyodrębnia segmenty interpolacji z expandowalnego stringu

member.name: T:PSParser.PSTokenizer
member.summary: Tokenizer dla PowerShela - rozpoznaje tokeny i obsługuje string interpolation

member.name: M:PSParser.PSTokenizer.Tokenize
member.summary: Tokenizuje kod i zwraca listę tokenów

member.name: M:PSParser.PSTokenizer.ScanExpandableString
member.summary: Skanuje expandowalny string (z $variables lub $(expressions))

member.name: M:PSParser.PSTokenizer.ScanInterpolationExpression
member.summary: Skanuje wyrażenie interpolacji w stringu (np. $variable lub $(expression))

member.name: T:PSParser.TokenType
member.summary: Reprezentuje typ tokenu w PowerShelu

member.name: T:PSParser.Token
member.summary: Reprezentuje pojedynczy token z kodu PowerShela
